name: Access Capital, Inc.
description_short: Access Capital is an independent lender specializing in payroll funding and asset-based lending exclusively for staffing companies, offering $1M-$25M credit facilities.
description_long:
Access Capital, Inc. is a specialized business lender based in New York that has built decades of experience financing the staffing industry. The company positions itself as the nation's leading independent lender to staffing firms, having financed hundreds of companies and deployed over $1 billion in capital to the sector. This deep vertical specialization differentiates them from generalist commercial lenders.

Access Capital offers three primary lending solutions: Payroll Funding (working capital for staffing operations), Asset-Based Lending, and M&A Financing for acquisitions. Their credit facilities range from $1 million to $25 million. The company emphasizes that their solutions are positioned between two extremes—less expensive and intrusive than factoring, while more flexible than traditional bank financing. They frame their value proposition around supporting growth-oriented staffing companies pursuing expansion, acquisitions, and business scaling.

What distinguishes Access Capital is their stated industry expertise and relationship-focused approach. Customer testimonials highlight flexibility, mentorship-style partnerships, and the company's ability to adapt support as clients scale. The company explicitly markets itself as understanding staffing industry dynamics in ways generalist lenders cannot, positioning specialized expertise as their competitive advantage over bank alternatives.

A realistic assessment notes that Access Capital operates in a niche market (staffing companies only) with relatively high minimum borrowing thresholds ($1M floor). While testimonials are positive, there is no pricing transparency on the website regarding APRs, fees, or rate structures. The company provides no information about approval timelines, credit requirements, or specific underwriting criteria, making it difficult for prospective borrowers to self-assess eligibility before contact.
address: 400 Park Ave Suite 1900, New York, NY 10022

company_info.headquarters: 400 Park Ave Suite 1900, New York, NY 10022

pros: Specializes exclusively in staffing industry lending with 20+ years documented experience
Offers credit facilities up to $25 million, suitable for mid-sized and larger staffing firms
Provides flexible working capital solutions positioned as less expensive than factoring
Demonstrates strong track record with case studies showing 300%+ growth outcomes for clients
Multiple lending products (payroll funding, asset-based lending, M&A financing) under one provider
Maintains dedicated staffing industry expertise and relationship management approach
No geographic restrictions indicated; serves staffing companies nationally
cons: Minimum loan amount of $1 million excludes small and early-stage staffing firms
No pricing, APR, fees, or rate information disclosed on website; requires direct inquiry
No information provided about approval timeline, credit score requirements, or underwriting standards
Niche focus on staffing industry only—not available to companies in other sectors
No online application or pre-qualification process; all inquiries require phone contact or meeting
best_for: Mid-sized staffing companies (revenues $5M+) seeking working capital for payroll expansion
Growth-stage staffing firms pursuing acquisitions or M&A transactions
Staffing companies looking for more flexible alternatives to traditional bank term loans or factoring
diagnosis: Access Capital is designed exclusively for mid-sized and larger staffing companies ($1M+ financing need) seeking specialized lending expertise and flexible capital solutions for payroll, growth, or acquisitions. The main caveat is lack of pricing transparency and high minimum loan thresholds that exclude smaller staffing operations; prospective borrowers must contact the company directly for rate quotes and eligibility assessment.
services: Asset-based lending secured by company assets
M&A financing for acquisitions and mergers
Credit facilities from $1 million to $25 million
